findings.evidence: Grep hits: `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/ClosureSwap.lean:125 theorem mem_closure_isSwap' {f : Perm α} : f ∈ closure {σ : Perm α | σ.IsSwap} ↔ (fixedBy α f)ᶜ.Finite`; `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/ClosureSwap.lean:61 theorem finite_compl_fixedBy_swap`; `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Support.lean:221 lemma set_support_symm_eq`; `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Support.lean:235 theorem set_support_mul_subset`.
findings.fix: Remove the new predicate/closure wrappers and index `exchangeableSigma` by the existing predicate `(MulAction.fixedBy ℕ π)ᶜ.Finite` or by `π ∈ Subgroup.closure {σ : Equiv.Perm ℕ | σ.IsSwap}`, using Mathlib’s equivalence when needed.
findings.issue: `IsFinitelySupportedPerm` and its basic API at lines 23, 54, 61, and 68 duplicate Mathlib’s established finite-support formulation for permutations as `(fixedBy α f)ᶜ.Finite`.

summary: The exchangeable σ-algebra and path-tail adapter appear new, but the PR introduces a parallel finite-supported-permutation API instead of using Mathlib’s existing formulation for permutation finite support.
